questions.text: How many pillars of Islam are there?
questions.explanation: Islam is built on 5 pillars: the testimony of faith, prayer, zakat, fasting, and pilgrimage.

questions.text: How many pillars of faith (Iman) are there?
questions.explanation: The 6 pillars of faith are: belief in Allah, His angels, His books, His messengers, the Last Day, and Divine Destiny.

questions.text: What is the first pillar of Islam?
questions.explanation: The testimony of faith (Shahada) is the gateway into Islam.

questions.text: Which prayer is performed in the middle of the day, just after the sun has passed its zenith?
questions.explanation: Dhuhr is the second obligatory prayer of the day.

questions.text: How many units of prayer (Rakats) does the Maghrib prayer (sunset) contain?
questions.explanation: The Maghrib prayer consists of 3 obligatory Rakats.

questions.text: How many units of prayer (Rakats) does the Fajr prayer (dawn) contain?
questions.explanation: The Fajr prayer consists of 2 Rakats.

questions.text: Which surah must be recited in every unit of prayer?
questions.explanation: Reciting Surah Al-Fatiha is a pillar of prayer; the prayer is not valid without it.

questions.text: What is the name of dry ablution performed with earth or stone?
questions.explanation: Tayammum replaces water when it is unavailable or cannot be used for medical reasons.

questions.text: Towards which city do Muslims face when praying (the Qibla)?
questions.explanation: The Qibla is the direction of the Kaaba, located in Mecca.

questions.text: Does laughing out loud invalidate the prayer?
questions.explanation: Loud laughter invalidates the prayer (and according to some opinions, the ablution as well).

questions.text: What is the greatest sin in Islam, considered the first nullifier?
questions.explanation: Shirk (associating partners with Allah) is the only sin Allah does not forgive if not followed by repentance before death.

questions.text: Is sorcery considered a nullifier of Islam?
questions.explanation: The practice of sorcery is an act of major disbelief.

questions.text: What is the ruling for someone who mocks an aspect of Allah's religion?
options: It is a nullifier of Islam
questions.correctAnswer: It is a nullifier of Islam
questions.explanation: Mocking Allah, His Prophet, or His signs takes one out of Islam (Surah At-Tawbah).

questions.text: What was the name of Prophet Muhammad's father?
questions.explanation: The Prophet's father was called Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib.

questions.text: What was the name of Prophet Muhammad's mother?
questions.explanation: His mother was Amina bint Wahb.

questions.text: Who was the paternal grandfather of the Prophet who took care of him after his mother's death?
questions.explanation: Abdul-Muttalib raised the Prophet after the death of his mother Amina.

questions.text: Which uncle of the Prophet protected him for years without ever converting to Islam?
questions.explanation: Abu Talib, Ali's father, protected his nephew Muhammad until his death, though he remained on the religion of his ancestors.

questions.text: Who was the first wife of Prophet Muhammad?
questions.explanation: Khadijah bint Khuwaylid was the Prophet's first wife and the first person to believe in his mission.

questions.text: Which of these women is a daughter of Prophet Muhammad?
questions.explanation: Fatima Zahra is one of the four daughters of the Prophet.

questions.text: How many sons did Prophet Muhammad have (all died in infancy)?
questions.explanation: He had 3 sons: Qasim, Abdullah, and Ibrahim.

questions.text: Who was the wet nurse of Prophet Muhammad?
questions.explanation: Halima, from the tribe of Banu Sa'd, was his wet nurse in the desert.

questions.text: To which tribe did Prophet Muhammad belong?
questions.explanation: The Prophet was part of the noble Quraysh tribe in Mecca.

questions.text: Who is the first man created by Allah and the first prophet?
questions.explanation: Adam is the father of humanity and the first prophet.

questions.text: Which prophet built the Ark to escape the Flood?
questions.explanation: Nuh built the Ark by Allah's command to save the believers and animals.

questions.text: Who is called 'Khalil Allah' (The Close Friend of Allah)?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim received this honorific title for his complete devotion.

questions.text: Which son of Ibrahim was to be sacrificed before Allah replaced him with a ram?
questions.explanation: According to Islamic tradition, it was Ismail who was the child of sacrifice.

questions.text: Which prophet was swallowed by a large fish (or whale)?
questions.explanation: Yunus (Dhun-Nun) was swallowed by the fish after leaving his people without permission.

questions.text: Which prophet spoke from the cradle to defend his mother Maryam (Mary)?
questions.explanation: Isa spoke miraculously at birth to exonerate his mother.

questions.text: Which prophet parted the Red Sea with his staff?
questions.explanation: Musa parted the sea by Allah's permission to save the Children of Israel from Pharaoh.

questions.text: Which prophet could understand the language of animals and command the Jinn?
questions.explanation: Allah granted Sulayman a vast kingdom and power over animals and Jinn.

questions.text: Which prophet was thrown into a well by his brothers?
questions.explanation: Yusuf was betrayed by his jealous brothers, thrown into a well, then sold in Egypt.

questions.text: Which holy book was revealed to Prophet Dawud (David)?
questions.explanation: The Zabur is the book revealed to Dawud.

questions.text: During which Islamic month do Muslims fast?
questions.explanation: Ramadan is the 9th month of the Hijri calendar and the month of obligatory fasting.

questions.text: What is the pre-dawn meal taken during Ramadan called?
questions.explanation: Suhoor is the blessed meal taken before the fast begins at dawn.

questions.text: What is the night during Ramadan that is better than 1000 months called?
questions.explanation: It was during this night that the Quran began to be revealed.

questions.text: Which festival marks the end of Ramadan?
questions.explanation: Eid al-Fitr is the festival of breaking the fast.

questions.text: What is the nightly group prayer performed during Ramadan called?
questions.explanation: Tarawih are the voluntary prayers performed after Isha prayer during Ramadan.

questions.text: What is the percentage of Zakat on money saved for one year?
questions.explanation: The rate of Zakat Al-Maal is generally 2.5%.

questions.text: What is the longest surah in the Quran?
questions.explanation: Surah Al-Baqara (The Cow) is the second surah and the longest.

questions.text: What is the shortest surah in the Quran?
questions.explanation: Surah Al-Kawthar (The Abundance) contains only 3 verses and is the shortest.

questions.text: Over how many years was the Quran revealed to the Prophet?
questions.explanation: The revelation lasted about 23 years (13 in Mecca and 10 in Medina).

questions.text: Which angel was responsible for transmitting the revelation to the Prophet?
questions.explanation: Angel Jibril is the angel of revelation.

questions.text: Where did the Prophet receive the first revelation?
questions.explanation: It was in the Cave of Hira, on Mount An-Nur, that the first verses were revealed.

questions.text: What were the first words revealed of the Quran?
questions.explanation: The first word revealed was 'Iqra' (Read/Recite) from Surah Al-Alaq.

questions.text: Which companion is nicknamed 'As-Siddiq' (The Truthful)?
questions.explanation: Abu Bakr received this title for his unwavering faith, particularly during the Night Journey episode.

questions.text: Which companion is nicknamed 'Al-Farooq' (The One Who Distinguishes Truth from Falsehood)?
questions.explanation: It was the Prophet who gave this nickname to Umar.

questions.text: Which of these men is NOT one of the 'Rightly Guided Caliphs' (Rashidun)?
questions.explanation: The four rightly guided caliphs are Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, and Ali.

questions.text: In which year of the Hijra did the great Battle of Badr take place?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Badr took place during Ramadan in the 2nd year of Hijra.

questions.text: Which prayer has no bowing (Ruku) or prostration (Sujud)?
questions.explanation: The funeral prayer is performed standing with 4 Takbirs.

questions.text: What is the name of the water source that sprang beneath Ismail's feet in Mecca?
questions.explanation: Zamzam water sprang forth to quench Hajar and her son Ismail.

questions.text: Which prophet is known for his legendary patience in the face of illness and loss of wealth?
questions.explanation: Ayyub is the Quranic example of patience (Sabr) in the face of trials.

questions.text: What is the name of the first mosque built by the Prophet in Medina (or just before entering)?
questions.explanation: Quba Mosque is the first mosque built in Islam.

questions.text: Is it permissible to fast on the day of Eid?
questions.explanation: It is strictly forbidden to fast on the days of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha.

questions.text: Which act marks the end of the state of consecration (Ihram) during Hajj or Umrah?
questions.explanation: Shaving or cutting hair (Tahallul) marks the exit from the state of Ihram.

questions.text: How many circuits around the Kaaba must be performed during Tawaf?
questions.explanation: Tawaf consists of 7 circuits around the Kaaba.

questions.text: Between which hills is the Sa'i (rapid walking) performed?
questions.explanation: Sa'i consists of 7 trips back and forth between the hills of Safa and Marwa.

questions.text: What is the most important day of the week for Muslims?
questions.explanation: Friday is the best day of the week upon which the sun has risen.

questions.text: What does the word 'Islam' mean?
questions.explanation: Although related to the root of peace, Islam technically means submission to the will of Allah.

questions.text: Who built the Kaaba with his son?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim and his son Ismail raised the foundations of the House (Kaaba).

questions.text: Which uncle of the Prophet is cursed in a surah of the Quran?
questions.explanation: Surah Al-Masad (Tabbat) was revealed concerning Abu Lahab and his wife.

questions.text: Which of these battles was a defeat for the Muslims due to the disobedience of the archers?
questions.explanation: At Uhud, the archers left their post thinking the battle was over, allowing the enemy to counterattack.

questions.text: Which prophet received the Torah?
questions.explanation: The Torah was revealed to Musa (Moses).

questions.text: Who is Pharaoh's wife cited as an example for believers in the Quran?
questions.explanation: Asiya bint Muzahim is Pharaoh's wife who believed in Musa and Allah.

questions.text: How many names of Allah (Asma ul-Husna) are generally mentioned in the famous hadith?
questions.explanation: The hadith mentions that Allah has 99 names; whoever memorizes them will enter Paradise.

questions.text: Which surah is equivalent to one-third of the Quran in recitation?
questions.explanation: Surah Al-Ikhlas (Pure Monotheism) is considered equivalent to one-third of the Quran.

questions.text: What is 'Ghusl'?
questions.explanation: Ghusl is the complete washing of the body necessary after a state of major impurity.

questions.text: Which voluntary prayer is strongly recommended in mid-morning?
questions.explanation: The Duha prayer is performed after sunrise and before the zenith.

questions.text: Must Muslims believe in the books revealed before the Quran (Torah, Gospel, Psalms) in their original form?
questions.explanation: It is a pillar of faith to believe that Allah revealed books to His prophets, although current versions may be altered.

questions.text: Who will be the companion of Prophet Isa at his return at the end of times?
questions.explanation: Isa will descend and pray behind the Mahdi according to prophetic tradition.

questions.text: Which angel is tasked with blowing the Trumpet on the Day of Judgment?
questions.explanation: Israfil is the angel designated to blow the Trumpet (As-Sur).

questions.text: What is the name of the guardian of Hell?
questions.explanation: Malik is the chief guardian of Hell.

questions.text: What is the name of the guardian of Paradise?
questions.explanation: Ridwan is traditionally known as the guardian of Paradise.

questions.text: Which city became the capital of the Islamic state after the Hijra?
questions.explanation: Medina became the center of the Muslim community after the Prophet's emigration.

questions.text: How many gates does Paradise have?
questions.explanation: Paradise has 8 gates, one of which is reserved for those who fast (Ar-Rayyan).

questions.text: What is the name of the gate of Paradise reserved for those who fast?
questions.explanation: Ar-Rayyan is the exclusive gate for those who fast.

questions.text: What is the name of the Prophet's mount during the Night Journey (Isra and Miraj)?
questions.explanation: Al-Buraq is the fantastic creature that transported the Prophet from Mecca to Jerusalem and then to the heavens.

questions.text: Which prophet has no father?
questions.explanation: Isa was born miraculously from the Virgin Mary without a father.

questions.text: Does Adam have a father and mother?
questions.explanation: Adam was created directly by Allah without father or mother.

questions.text: Which woman is considered one of the 4 best women in the universe and the daughter of Imran?
questions.explanation: Maryam bint Imran is one of the perfect women according to tradition.

questions.text: Which caliph ordered the final compilation of the Quran into one standardized book?
questions.explanation: It was under the caliphate of Uthman ibn Affan that the Quran was standardized and copied.

questions.text: What is 'Sayyid al-Istighfar'?
options: The best formula for seeking forgiveness
questions.correctAnswer: The best formula for seeking forgiveness
questions.explanation: It is a specific supplication taught by the Prophet for asking Allah's forgiveness.

questions.text: What is the 'Tashahhud'?
options: The recitation made while sitting in prayer
questions.correctAnswer: The recitation made while sitting in prayer
questions.explanation: The Tashahhud is recited in the sitting position (Jalsa) during prayer.

questions.text: Which battle was the first great victory of the Muslims?
questions.explanation: Badr is the first decisive victory.

questions.text: Which prophet was raised to the heavens and did not die?
questions.explanation: According to the Quran, Isa was neither killed nor crucified, but raised to Allah.

questions.text: How many times per day do Muslims prostrate at minimum in obligatory prayers?
questions.explanation: There are 17 obligatory Rakats per day, and each Rakat contains 2 prostrations: 17 * 2 = 34.

questions.text: What is the name of the Angel of Death?
questions.explanation: Although the name Azrael is common in tradition, the Quran calls him 'The Angel of Death' (Malak al-Mawt).

questions.text: Which surah is called 'The Heart of the Quran'?
questions.explanation: Surah Yasin is often designated as such in traditions.

questions.text: Which surah protects from the punishment of the grave if recited every night?
questions.explanation: Surah Al-Mulk (The Sovereignty) is protective against the torment of the grave.

questions.text: Which surah is recommended to read on Fridays?
questions.explanation: Reading Surah Al-Kahf on Friday illuminates the believer with light until the following Friday.

questions.text: What is the length of a day with Allah according to the Quran (in some verses)?
questions.explanation: The Quran mentions that a day with Him is like 1000 years of what you count (Surah Al-Hajj).

questions.text: Which prophet was sent to the people of Thamud?
questions.explanation: Prophet Salih was sent to the Thamud with the miracle of the she-camel.

questions.text: Which prophet was sent to the people of 'Ad?
questions.explanation: Prophet Hud was sent to the 'Ad.

questions.text: Which prophet was sent to Madyan?
questions.explanation: Shu'ayb preached to the people of Madyan who cheated in weights and measures.

questions.text: Who is the only woman mentioned by her first name in the Quran?
questions.explanation: Only Maryam (mother of Jesus) is mentioned by name. Others are referred to by their relationship (wife of, mother of...).

questions.text: Who killed Jalut (Goliath)?
questions.explanation: Dawud, then young, killed the giant Jalut with his sling.

questions.text: Which companion had the most beautiful voice for reciting the Quran, whom the Prophet said had received 'a flute from the family of David'?
questions.explanation: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari was renowned for his magnificent recitation.

questions.text: Who was the first muezzin of Islam?
questions.explanation: Bilal, the former Abyssinian slave, was chosen by the Prophet to make the call to prayer.

questions.text: What is 'Dhikr'?
options: The remembrance and mention of Allah
questions.correctAnswer: The remembrance and mention of Allah
questions.explanation: Dhikr encompasses all forms of remembering Allah (SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, etc.).

questions.text: In which city is the Prophet buried?
questions.explanation: He is buried in his mosque in Medina.

questions.text: What is the definition of Ihsan (Excellence) according to the hadith of Jibril?
options: Worshipping Allah as if you see Him
questions.correctAnswer: Worshipping Allah as if you see Him
questions.explanation: Ihsan is to worship Allah as if you see Him, for if you do not see Him, He sees you.

questions.text: What is 'Witr'?
options: An odd-numbered prayer performed at night
questions.correctAnswer: An odd-numbered prayer performed at night
questions.explanation: Witr is the last prayer of the night, consisting of an odd number of Rakats (often 1 or 3).

questions.text: Who are the 'People of the Book' (Ahl al-Kitab)?
questions.explanation: This term refers to those who received revealed scriptures before the Quran.

questions.text: What is the reward for an accepted Hajj (Mabrur)?
questions.explanation: The Prophet said that an accepted Hajj has no reward other than Paradise.

questions.text: Which wife of the Prophet reported the most hadiths?
questions.explanation: Aisha is one of the greatest transmitters of knowledge in Islam.

questions.text: How old was the Prophet at his death?
questions.explanation: He died at 63 years old.

questions.text: How old was the Prophet at the first revelation?
questions.explanation: The prophetic mission began at his 40 years of age.

questions.text: Which cousin of the Prophet slept in his bed on the night of the Hijra to deceive the assassins?
questions.explanation: Ali ibn Abi Talib took the Prophet's place in his bed, risking his life.

questions.text: Is it permissible to pray exactly at sunrise?
questions.explanation: It is forbidden to pray exactly at sunrise, zenith, and sunset.

questions.text: What is 'Zakat al-Fitr'?
options: Charity at the end of Ramadan
questions.correctAnswer: Charity at the end of Ramadan
questions.explanation: It is a charity (usually in food) given before Eid al-Fitr prayer to purify the fasting person.

questions.text: In which direction should a deceased Muslim be positioned in the grave?
questions.explanation: The body is placed on its right side, with the face turned towards the Qibla (Mecca).
